startup_profile.name: Synapse BridgED
startup_profile.problem_summary: A B2B platform bridging the gap between engineering classroom theory and real-world application through active, lab-linked projects.

problem_statement.core_issue: India produces over a million engineering graduates annually, yet employability hovers between 45-55%.
problem_statement.context: While the startup ecosystem is booming, students lack the conceptual foundation to turn learning into innovation.

supporting_data.insight: Innovation is rising, but conceptual understanding is lagging. Only a small fraction of students can apply classroom theory.
supporting_data.data_point: Employability peaked at ~15.9% for high-skilled roles in 2025.
Startups grew from 30,000 (2019) to over 1.8 lakh (2025).

supporting_data.insight: Low R&D investment limits hands-on inquiry.
supporting_data.data_point: India spends only ~0.64% of GDP on R&D, resulting in fewer well-funded labs.
Low funding means teaching staff rarely engage in funded research, leading to rote-based instruction.

customer_validation.methodology: 16 offline interviews and 162 online responses from PES students.
customer_validation.key_pain_point: Students struggle with new topics due to forgotten prerequisites or missed classes.
customer_validation.student_quote: I missed one class, and it took me three hours on YouTube to find what my teacher explained in ten minutes.

quantified_loss.monetary_loss_per_semester: ₹47,000 (₹40,000 in lost time + ₹7,000 on external courses)

direct_competitors: Generic Online Courses (No link to college syllabus/creative projects)
competitive_landscape.differentiation: Unlike generic apps, Synapse BridgED fits into the specific college routine and links directly to lab work.

market_opportunity.gap: Existing tools dump information without connecting it to classwork.
market_opportunity.value_proposition: Building a bridge between classroom teaching and real understanding to make learning simpler, not heavier.

market_size.industry_growth: India EdTech market at $7.5B, projected $25-33B by 2030 (CAGR 17-28%).

business_model.revenue_stream: Platform licenses sold directly to colleges.
